rules.when: The task depends on fresh news, current events, live public facts, or recent market and product changes.
rules.why: Grok is the preferred dispatch when current web-connected context is central to the work.

rules.when: The task is very simple and low risk: rote file gathering, backlog/report grooming, tiny wording changes, trivial formatting, or cheap parallel analysis with no credentials, production runtime, money impact, destructive git, or external side effects.
rules.why: Prefer MiniMax over GPT-5.6-Luna when the work is simple enough and conserving Codex tokens matters.

rules.when: The task is small but should stay on Codex: focused code inspection, low-risk local tooling, narrow tests, or simple work that benefits from Codex repo context.
rules.why: Use GPT-5.6-Luna when Codex is useful but the task does not need the balanced or frontier profile.

rules.when: The task is normal implementation, a focused bug fix, tests, local tooling updates, Firstmate operational changes, or everyday backlog work.
rules.why: Use GPT-5.6-Terra as the balanced everyday Codex default.

rules.when: The task is production/runtime/debugging work, JT refresh/replenishment/PPC/SellerSnap work, credentials/auth, security, destructive git, money-impacting operations, broad multi-file implementation, complex integration work, or ambiguous high-risk work.
rules.why: Use GPT-5.6-Sol when mistakes are expensive or the work touches live operations.

rules.when: The task is a critical incident, repeated failure after previous fixes, architecture decision, memory/router policy, irreversible action planning, large cross-system migration, or work where a wrong answer could mislead several future agents.
rules.why: Reserve GPT-5.6-Sol xhigh for the highest-consequence work.
